subject: Ecology
title: Nutrient Poverty in Rich Forests
description: A quick win: understand Nutrient Poverty in Rich Forests.

hook.text: The world's richest forests grow on some of the world's poorest soils.

buildup.text: Heavy tropical rains leach minerals out of the soil constantly.

discovery.text: Nutrients are locked in living biomass. Decomposition happens so fast that nothing accumulates.

twist.text: Clear a rainforest and the exposed soil becomes useless for farming within a few years.

climax.text: The forest sustains itself by recycling everything instantly—soil is just a middleman.

punchline.text: The trees are the nutrient bank, not the soil.

quiz.question: Where are most nutrients stored in a tropical rainforest?
options: In living biomass, not in the soil
